title: Hanging a bifold door
subtitle: Fit a two-panel folding door in a wardrobe or cupboard opening
excerpt: How to install a bifold door set in a standard opening: cutting and fixing the overhead track, fitting the pivot cup and top guide, hanging the panels, and adjusting the alignment so the door folds flat and the latch engages cleanly.
sourceNotes: Collins Complete DIY Manual. Reader's Digest Complete DIY Manual.

glossaryTerms.definition: The overhead aluminium or steel channel that guides the top roller of a bifold door. The track carries the door's weight through the top pivot and keeps the door in alignment through the full fold cycle.
The bottom fitting that holds the floor pivot pin of the door's fixed panel. The cup is adjustable in height and lateral position, and provides the primary alignment point for the whole door set.

content.text: A bifold door folds in the middle; one panel is hinged to the frame at the bottom and runs in a

at the floor is the primary alignment point; the top track must be perfectly level or the door will bind. Most kit sets are self-contained; follow the kit's specific pivot spacing before cutting the track.
Step 1, measure and cut the track

Measure the opening width precisely. Cut the bifold track to length with a tenon saw or hacksaw (aluminium track); a fine-tooth blade avoids burring the channel. The track should be 2 to 3 mm shorter than the opening width to allow for the pivot cup fitting. Mark the track's pivot-bracket position from the frame edge (stated in the kit instructions, typically 20 to 25 mm from the hinge side of the opening).

Hold the track against the head of the opening and check it is level with the spirit level. The track must be truly level, not parallel to an out-of-level opening head. Drill pilot holes and fix the track with the screws supplied, going into the door head timber, not just into plasterboard. Check level again after fixing; the first screw can pull the track slightly.
Step 3, fit the pivot cup and top pivot bracket

Fix the pivot cup to the floor directly below the track's pivot bracket position. The cup must be plumb below the bracket; use a plumb line or digital level rather than estimating. Fix the top pivot bracket to the hinge-side door jamb at the height specified in the kit. The bracket usually has a 5 to 10 mm adjustment range in all directions, which is the tolerance for the plumb-line alignment.

Fit the top roller into the track channel and lower the bottom pivot pin into the pivot cup. The roller slides in the track; the pivot cup accepts the pin at the foot of the fixed panel. With most kits the panels are hinged together before hanging; check the kit sequence. Once both pivot points are engaged, test the fold: the door should open and close in a smooth arc without lifting out of the pivot cup.

Adjust the pivot cup height to set the door's bottom clearance above the floor (typically 5 to 8 mm). Adjust the lateral position of the cup to close any gap between the door edge and the frame. Most top pivot brackets also have a height adjuster; use it to level the top edge of the door. The two panels should fold flat and parallel when the door is fully open.

attrs.symptom: Door drags on the floor when opening
attrs.cause: Pivot cup height is set too low, or the floor is uneven and rises toward the open position.
attrs.fix: Raise the pivot cup height by 2 to 3 mm. If the floor rises, plane the foot of the door to clear the high point while maintaining the correct clearance at the pivot.

attrs.symptom: Door will not fold flat; panels splay outward at the centre hinge
attrs.cause: The top pivot bracket is set too far into the opening relative to the bottom pivot cup, or the panels are not exactly the same width.
attrs.fix: Adjust the lateral position of the top pivot bracket to bring the pivot points into exact vertical alignment. Check the panel widths are equal before adjusting the hardware.

attrs.symptom: Top roller jumps out of the track during operation
attrs.cause: The track retaining flange is not fully closing over the roller, or the roller is undersized for the track.
attrs.fix: Check the track retaining cap is clipped in place (many tracks ship without the retaining end cap fitted). Confirm the roller and track are from the same kit.
